title: Crunchy Spicy Spanish Tofu Croquettes
description: Fiery, crisp tofu croquettes with a hint of apple juice, perfect for a cozy Spanish-inspired snack.
instructions: Preheat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat until shimmering.

In a mixing bowl combine crumbled tofu, almond flour, chopped onion, minced garlic, smoked paprika, cayenne pepper, cumin, and salt. Pour in apple juice and stir until the mixture holds together. Let sit for 5 minutes to thicken.
Shape the tofu mixture into 1.5‑inch balls, using your palms to smooth the surface.
Coat each ball in panko breadcrumbs, pressing gently so the crumbs adhere. Place coated croquettes on a plate and set aside.
Heat the skillet to about 170 °C (340 °F). Fry the croquettes 3–4 minutes per side, or until golden and crispy. Remove and drain on paper towels.
Serve the hot croquettes with a squeeze of fresh lemon or a drizzle of homemade spicy aioli for extra flavor.
If storing, keep refrigerated (up to 2 days). Reheat in a preheated oven at 200 °C for 10 minutes before serving.
Enjoy the crunchy, spicy Spanish tofu croquettes as a comforting snack or light meal.
